vocabulary.meaning: a person or organization that employs people
vocabulary.example: The factory is the largest employer in the area.

vocabulary.meaning: a person employed for wages or salary, especially at non-executive level
vocabulary.example: The company has over five hundred employees.

vocabulary.meaning: all the people employed by a particular organization
vocabulary.example: The school has a very friendly and dedicated teaching staff.

vocabulary.meaning: a paid position of regular employment
vocabulary.example: She applied for a job at the local bank.

vocabulary.meaning: activity involving mental or physical effort done in order to achieve a purpose or result
vocabulary.example: He has a lot of work to finish before tomorrow.

vocabulary.meaning: an occupation undertaken for a significant period of a person's life and with opportunities for progress
vocabulary.example: She is planning a career in journalism.

vocabulary.meaning: obtain money in return for labor or services
vocabulary.example: How much does she earn in her new job?

vocabulary.meaning: be successful or victorious in a contest or conflict
vocabulary.example: He won the employee of the month award.

vocabulary.meaning: obtain or secure something wanted or desirable
vocabulary.example: She has gained valuable experience working as an intern.

vocabulary.meaning: increase the amount, level, or strength of
vocabulary.example: The company raised the salary of all its employees by five percent.

vocabulary.meaning: move from a lower position to a higher one; increase in amount, size, or value
vocabulary.example: The cost of living has risen significantly this year.

vocabulary.meaning: a fixed regular payment, typically paid on a daily or weekly basis, to a manual or unskilled worker
vocabulary.example: He earns a decent weekly wage working construction.

vocabulary.meaning: a fixed regular payment, typically paid on a monthly basis but often expressed as an annual sum, made by an employer to an employee
vocabulary.example: She receives an annual salary of forty thousand dollars.

vocabulary.meaning: money paid to someone for regular work
vocabulary.example: The workers are demanding better pay and conditions.

vocabulary.meaning: travel some distance between one's home and place of work on a regular basis
vocabulary.example: She commutes to work by train every day.

vocabulary.meaning: bring and hand over a letter, parcel, or goods to the proper recipient or address
vocabulary.example: The courier delivered the package this morning.

vocabulary.meaning: leave one's job and cease to work, typically upon reaching the normal age for retirement
vocabulary.example: He retired from teaching at the age of sixty-five.

vocabulary.meaning: voluntarily leave a job or other position
vocabulary.example: She resigned from the committee after the disagreement.

vocabulary.meaning: dismiss an employee from a job
vocabulary.example: He was fired for consistently arriving late.

vocabulary.meaning: dismiss from employment
vocabulary.example: They sacked him because of poor performance.

vocabulary.meaning: dismiss a person because their job is no longer needed
vocabulary.example: Fifty workers were made redundant when the factory closed.

vocabulary.meaning: time worked in addition to one's normal working hours
vocabulary.example: She worked a lot of overtime last week to finish the report.

vocabulary.meaning: advancement of an employee's position within the organization
vocabulary.example: She got a promotion to senior manager.

vocabulary.meaning: a regular payment made during retirement from an investment fund
vocabulary.example: He is living on his state pension.

vocabulary.meaning: a commercial business
vocabulary.example: She works for a software company.

vocabulary.meaning: a business concern, especially one involving a partnership of two or more people
vocabulary.example: He is a partner in a local law firm.

vocabulary.meaning: a person's regular occupation, profession, or trade; or commercial activity
vocabulary.example: They are planning to start their own business.

vocabulary.meaning: an organized association of workers formed to protect and further their rights and interests
vocabulary.example: The trade union called a strike over pay disputes.

vocabulary.meaning: an organization set up to provide help and raise money for those in need
vocabulary.example: All the proceeds from the concert went to charity.

vocabulary.meaning: a job or profession
vocabulary.example: Please state your name, age, and occupation.

vocabulary.meaning: a refusal to work organized by a body of employees as a form of protest
vocabulary.example: Train drivers are on strike today.

vocabulary.meaning: a media company that operates in several different countries
vocabulary.example: He got a job at a multinational media organisation.

vocabulary.meaning: a period of temporary economic decline during which trade and industrial activity are reduced
vocabulary.example: Many small businesses closed down during the recession.

vocabulary.meaning: at the beginning
vocabulary.example: Initially, the project was funded by a local grant.

vocabulary.meaning: the quality of being dedicated or committed to a task or purpose
vocabulary.example: She was awarded a medal for her dedication to duty.

vocabulary.meaning: the state or quality of being dedicated to a cause, activity, etc.
vocabulary.example: The company's commitment to quality is well known.

vocabulary.meaning: a person or company that sells goods in large quantities, typically to retailers
vocabulary.example: They buy their stock directly from wholesalers.

vocabulary.meaning: the branch of medicine that deals with the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease in animals
vocabulary.example: She decided to study veterinary medicine at university.

vocabulary.meaning: a gymnast who performs rolls and swings on a trapeze
vocabulary.example: The crowd watched in awe as the trapeze artist performed a triple flip.

vocabulary.meaning: the ability to assess and initiate things independently, or a fresh approach
vocabulary.example: She showed great initiative in solving the customer's problem.

vocabulary.meaning: a plane that flies faster than the speed of sound
vocabulary.example: The Concorde was a famous supersonic plane.

vocabulary.meaning: argue about something; discuss heatedly
vocabulary.example: The two neighbors disputed the boundary line between their properties.

vocabulary.meaning: a sports competition involving tennis players
vocabulary.example: She won the local tennis tournament last weekend.

vocabulary.meaning: attractive in an exciting and special way
vocabulary.example: Being a model sounds like a glamorous job, but it is hard work.

vocabulary.meaning: stay in several places for only a short time, with all one's belongings in a suitcase
vocabulary.example: As a traveling salesman, he has to live out of a suitcase.

vocabulary.meaning: a device used for examining or exploring
vocabulary.example: The space probe sent back detailed images of Mars.

vocabulary.meaning: the quality of moving or happening at a low speed
vocabulary.example: The slowness of the computer is very frustrating.

vocabulary.meaning: a failure to function in a normal or satisfactory manner
vocabulary.example: The crash was caused by a technical malfunction.

vocabulary.meaning: failing to function normally or correctly
vocabulary.example: She returned the malfunctioning phone to the shop.

vocabulary.meaning: extremely bad or unpleasant
vocabulary.example: The weather was awful all weekend.

vocabulary.meaning: cooked until dry, hard, and easily broken, with a brown color
vocabulary.example: Bake the potatoes until they are crispy brown.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: decide not to do something that you had agreed to do
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: They agreed to buy the house but backed out at the last minute.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: produce and start to sell a new product
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The band is bringing out a new album next month.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: stop operating (for a company, shop, etc.)
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The local shop closed down last week because of high rents.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: recognise that sth is not true; continue or support sb until the end of a difficult period
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I saw through his lies immediately. / The loan should see them through to the end of the year.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: deal with or take care of something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Don't worry about the cooking, I'll see to it.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: start doing something eagerly and with determination
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We set to and cleaned the whole house in two hours.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: start working on a project with a specific goal in mind
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: They set out early in the morning to finish the garden.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: start a business, organization, or system
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She set up her own consulting firm last year.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: move or happen at a lower speed
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You need to slow down when driving in the rain.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: move or happen at a higher speed
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The train sped up as it left the station.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: take someone's place or do their job for a short time
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She stood in for the manager while he was on holiday.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: employ someone, or accept a job or responsibility
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The company is taking on new staff. / I can't take on any more work right now.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: take control of something (like a business)
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: A large multinational company took over their firm.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: refuse an offer, invitation, or request
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She turned down the job offer because the salary was too low.

collocations.meaning: reach a decision that everyone agrees on
arrive at a mutual decision after discussion
sharing the same opinion about something
organize plans with someone or for someone

collocations.collocation: have an arrangement with sb to do
collocations.meaning: possess a plan or deal with someone to do something
engage in commercial activity with someone
operating as a commercial enterprise
travel to a place for work purposes
a journey made for business purposes
small or large scale commercial enterprises

collocations.collocation: have a complaint (about) sth to sb
collocations.meaning: possess a grievance about something to report to someone

collocations.collocation: make a complaint (about) sth to sb
collocations.meaning: formally report a grievance to someone

collocations.collocation: letter of complaint to sb about sth
collocations.meaning: a formal written message reporting a grievance
take a day of holiday or rest from work
one's regular, main job, especially as opposed to a temporary or hobby job
a journey completed within one day
gradually or progressively each day
a recently past day, or the day following
choose to perform a particular action
commit to a choice or course of action
reach a final choice after consideration
arrive at a final choice after consideration
perform one's required task or obligation
a feeling of moral or legal obligation
actively working or performing one's role
not active at work or free from official duties

collocations.collocation: have a duty to sb to do
collocations.meaning: be morally or legally obligated to do something for someone
try hard to perform an action
devote time and energy to a task
live through or encounter an event
acquire skill or knowledge from practice
skilled or knowledgeable in a field
make a telephone caller wait
keep holding or keep possession of something
grasp or carry something

collocations.collocation: have an interest in sth / doing
collocations.meaning: possess a feeling of wanting to know or learn about something

collocations.collocation: take an interest in sth / doing
collocations.meaning: become concerned or curious about something

collocations.collocation: express an interest in sth / doing
collocations.meaning: state or show that one is curious about something
beneficial to you to perform a task
receive extra money from savings or investments
receive profit from money invested
spend extra money for borrowing money

a formal meeting to evaluate an applicant for a job

possess a task that needs completion

possess tasks that require effort
travel to one's workplace
engaged in one's job at the workplace

unemployed
the location where one does their job

wordPatterns.meaning: not present in a place or event where you are expected to be
wordPatterns.example: EX: He was absent from work yesterday due to illness.

wordPatterns.meaning: formally request something, such as a job or permission
wordPatterns.example: EX: She decided to apply for the position of marketing assistant.

wordPatterns.meaning: make a formal request by sending a letter or email
wordPatterns.example: EX: Candidates must apply in writing before the deadline.

wordPatterns.meaning: join or fasten something to another thing
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please attach your resume to the application email.

wordPatterns.meaning: joined to something, or feeling a strong connection to something
wordPatterns.example: EX: The document is attached to the email. / She is very attached to her old laptop.

wordPatterns.meaning: start performing an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: We will begin working on the project tomorrow.

wordPatterns.meaning: start performing an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: I began to realize that the job was harder than I thought.

wordPatterns.meaning: start something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She began her career as a junior clerk.

wordPatterns.meaning: start an activity with a particular action
wordPatterns.example: EX: He began by thanking everyone for their support.

wordPatterns.meaning: rely on or be controlled by something or someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: Our success depends on how hard we work.

wordPatterns.meaning: having skill or knowledge because you have done something for a long time
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is highly experienced in managing large teams.

wordPatterns.verb: good for sb to do sth
wordPatterns.meaning: beneficial or helpful for someone to perform a task
wordPatterns.example: EX: It is good for you to gain some work experience during summer.

wordPatterns.meaning: able to do something well
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is very good at coding in Javascript.

wordPatterns.meaning: kind or helpful to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: The manager has been very good to me since I joined.

wordPatterns.meaning: pass exams or gain skills to do a particular job
wordPatterns.example: EX: She qualified as a teacher last year. / He qualified in veterinary medicine.

wordPatterns.meaning: having control over or care for someone or something, as part of one's job or role
wordPatterns.example: EX: You will be responsible for coordinating the marketing campaign.

wordPatterns.meaning: limit one's activities or study to a particular subject or area
wordPatterns.example: EX: The firm specialises in corporate law.

wordPatterns.meaning: prepare oneself or be prepared through instruction for a job or activity
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is training to become a professional pilot.

wordPatterns.meaning: perform the duties of a particular job
wordPatterns.example: EX: He works as a software developer.

wordPatterns.meaning: do a job at a specific location or organization
wordPatterns.example: EX: She works at the bank on High Street.

wordPatterns.meaning: do a job in a specific industry or department
wordPatterns.example: EX: He works in the retail sector.

wordPatterns.meaning: be employed by a person or company
wordPatterns.example: EX: She works for a multinational media organisation.

wordFormation.meaning: add: join or put together to increase the size or number / addition: the action of adding / added: extra, or thặng dư / additional: extra or supplementary / additionally: in addition
wordFormation.example:
EX: Add the numbers together.
EX: The new building is a great addition to the school.
EX: Value added tax is applied here.
EX: There will be an additional charge for delivery.
EX: Additionally, we offer free support.

wordFormation.meaning: apply: request or make use of / applicant: a person who applies / application: a formal request / (in)applicable: (not) suitable or relevant / applied: put to practical use
wordFormation.example:
EX: You should apply for the job.
EX: There were over fifty applicants for the role.
EX: Submit your application online.
EX: The rule is not applicable in this case.
EX: He is studying applied mathematics.

wordFormation.meaning: commerce: the activity of buying and selling / commercial: relating to buying and selling / commercially: in a commercial way
wordFormation.example:
EX: Online commerce has grown rapidly.
EX: The commercial break lasted for five minutes.
EX: The product is not yet commercially available.

wordFormation.meaning: dedicate: devote time or effort to / dedication: the quality of being committed / dedicated: devoted or loyal
wordFormation.example:
EX: He dedicated his book to his wife.
EX: She is known for her dedication to her work.
EX: We have a dedicated team of customer support representatives.

wordFormation.meaning: effect: a change which is a result of an action / (in)effective: (not) producing the desired result
wordFormation.example:
EX: The new law had a major effect on businesses.
EX: The new regulations were highly effective.

wordFormation.meaning: employ: give work to and pay for it / employer: a person or company that employs people / employee: a person employed / (un)employment: state of having or not having a job / (un)employable: (not) fit or suitable to be employed / (un)employed: (not) having a paid job
wordFormation.example:
EX: The company employs two hundred workers.
EX: Her employer offered her a raise.
EX: Employees are required to wear uniforms.
EX: The government is trying to reduce unemployment.
EX: Without qualifications, he is virtually unemployable.
EX: He has been unemployed for three months.

wordFormation.meaning: help: make it easier for someone to do something / helper: a person who helps / helping: a portion of food / (un)helpful: (not) providing assistance / helpless: unable to defend oneself or act without help
wordFormation.example:
EX: Can you help me move this desk?
EX: The helpers set up the chairs for the event.
EX: He had a second helping of pie.
EX: The staff were very helpful.
EX: The lost puppy looked small and helpless.

wordFormation.meaning: industry: economic activity concerned with processing raw materials and manufacture / industrial: relating to industry / industrious: hard-working
wordFormation.example:
EX: The manufacturing industry is recovering.
EX: They live in an industrial area.
EX: She is an industrious student who always finishes her homework.

wordFormation.meaning: machine: an apparatus using mechanical power / machinery: machines collectively
wordFormation.example:
EX: The washing machine is broken.
EX: The factory has imported new machinery.

wordFormation.meaning: manage: be in charge of / management: the process of dealing with or controlling things / manager: a person in charge / managing: having executive control
wordFormation.example:
EX: She manages a team of programmers.
EX: The company has excellent management.
EX: He reported the issue to the branch manager.
EX: She is the managing director of the firm.

wordFormation.meaning: profession: a paid occupation, especially one that involves prolonged training / professor: a university teacher of the highest rank / professionalism: the competence or skill expected of a professional / (un)professional: (not) worthy of or conforming to the standards of a profession
wordFormation.example:
EX: He is a lawyer by profession.
EX: The professor gave a lecture on economics.
EX: The client was impressed by her professionalism.
EX: It is unprofessional to arrive late for meetings.

wordFormation.meaning: meet: come together with someone / meeting: an assembly of people for discussion
wordFormation.example:
EX: Let's meet in the lobby at 9 a.m.
EX: The weekly meeting was postponed.

wordFormation.meaning: (un)qualified: (not) officially recognized as practitioner of a profession / qualifying: serving to qualify / qualification: official certificate of completion or requirement
wordFormation.example:
EX: She is a fully qualified accountant.
EX: The qualifying round of the tournament begins tomorrow.
EX: You need the right qualifications for this job.

wordFormation.meaning: responsible: having an obligation to do something / (ir)responsibility: (lack of) responsibility / irresponsible: not showing proper care
wordFormation.example:
EX: She is responsible for the project's budget.
EX: It is your responsibility to lock the office.
EX: It was irresponsible of him to leave the door unlocked.

wordFormation.meaning: supervise: observe and direct the execution of a task / supervisor: a person who supervises / supervision: the action of supervising
wordFormation.example:
EX: The manager supervises the training of new staff.
EX: Talk to your supervisor if you have any questions.
EX: The children played under the supervision of their teacher.

wordFormation.meaning: work: activity involving mental or physical effort / worker: a person who works / working: functioning or relating to work / (un)workable: (not) practical or feasible
wordFormation.example:
EX: He works long hours.
EX: The construction workers did a great job.
EX: She has a good working relationship with her boss.
EX: The proposed schedule is unworkable.
